00051| ### Models (synthesized understanding)
00052| - Daemon Autonomous Operation
00053|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/daemon-autonomous-operation.md
00054|   - Summary:
00055|     The daemon is an **autonomous agent spawner** that operates in a **poll-spawn-complete cycle**: polls beads for `triage:ready` issues, infers skill from issue type, spawns within capacity limits, monitors for `Phase: Complete`, verifies and closes. The daemon operates **independently of orchestrators** - orchestrators triage (label issues ready), daemon spawns (batch processing), orchestrators synthesize (review completed work). Skill inference uses **issue type** (not labels) to map task→investigation, bug→systematic-debugging, etc. Capacity management uses **WorkerPool** with reconciliation against OpenCode to free stale slots.
00056|     
00057|     ---
00058|   - Why This Fails:
00059|     ### 1. Capacity Starvation
00060|     
00061|     **What happens:** Pool shows MaxAgents active, but `orch status` shows fewer actual agents running.
00062|     
00063|     **Root cause:** Spawn failures don't release slots. Agent spawned, counted against pool, but spawn fails (bad skill name, missing context, etc.) - slot never released.
00064|     
00065|     **Why detection is hard:** Pool only knows about attempts, not outcomes. No feedback loop from spawn failure to pool.
00066|     
00067|     **Fix:** Reconciliation with OpenCode. Query actual sessions, release slots for non-existent agents.
00068|     
00069|     **Prevention:** Spawn tracking with retry limits (`pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`).
00070|     
00071|     ### 2. Duplicate Spawns
00072|     
00073|     **What happens:** Same issue spawned multiple times by daemon on consecutive polls.
00074|     
00075|     **Root cause:** Spawn latency. Issue labeled `triage:ready` at poll N, daemon spawns, but spawn hasn't transitioned issue to `in_progress` by poll N+1. Daemon sees same issue still ready, spawns again.
00076|     
00077|     **Why detection is hard:** Race condition between poll interval (60s) and spawn transition time (variable).
00078|     
00079|     **Fix:** Spawn deduplication via tracking. Track spawned beads IDs in memory, skip on subsequent polls until status confirms transition.
00080|     
00081|     **Source:** `pkg/daemon/spawn_tracker.go`
00082|     
00083|     ### 3. Skill Inference Mismatch
00084|     
00085|     **What happens:** Daemon spawns wrong skill for issue type.
00086|     
00087|     **Root cause:** Issue type doesn't match actual work needed. User creates `task` for implementation work (should be `feature`), daemon infers `investigation`.
00088|     
00089|     **Why detection is hard:** Type is set at creation, can't be changed by daemon. Daemon trusts type.
00090|     
00091|     **Fix:** Manual override. Orchestrator updates issue type before labeling `triage:ready`, or spawns manually with correct skill.
00092|     
00093|     **Prevention:** Better issue creation prompts, type validation, skill override via labels (future).
00094|

00162| - Orchestrator Session Lifecycle
00163|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md
00164|   - Summary:
00165|     Orchestrator sessions operate in a **three-tier hierarchy** (meta-orchestrator → orchestrator → worker) where each level is completed by the level above. Orchestrators produce **SESSION_HANDOFF.md** (not SYNTHESIS.md) and **wait** for completion (not /exit). They track via **session registry** (not beads) because orchestrators manage conversations, not work items. Frame collapse occurs when orchestrators drop levels and do work below their station - detected externally, not self-diagnosed. Checkpoint discipline uses duration thresholds (2h/3h/4h) as a proxy for context exhaustion.
00166|     
00167|     ---
00168|   - Why This Fails:
00169|     ### 1. Frame Collapse (Orchestrator → Worker)
00170|     
00171|     **What happens:** Orchestrator drops into worker-level implementation (editing code, debugging, investigating).
00172|     
00173|     **Root cause:** Vague goals → exploration mode → investigation → debugging. **Framing cues override skill instructions.**
00174|     
00175|     **Why detection is hard:** Orchestrators can't self-diagnose frame collapse. The frame defines what's visible, so from inside the collapsed frame, the behavior feels appropriate.
00176|     
00177|     **Detection signals:**
00178|     - Edit tool usage on code files (not orchestration artifacts)
00179|     - Time spent >15 minutes on direct fixes
00180|     - SESSION_HANDOFF.md shows "Manual fixes" sections
00181|     - Post-mortem reveals work that should have been spawned
00182|     
00183|     **NOT the fix:** Adding more ABSOLUTE DELEGATION RULE warnings. The agent already knows. The problem is framing, not awareness.
00184|     
00185|     **Prevention:**
00186|     1. Provide specific goals with action verbs, concrete deliverables, success criteria
00187|     2. Use WHICH vs HOW test: meta decides WHICH focus, orchestrator decides HOW to execute
00188|     3. Frame collapse check in SESSION_HANDOFF.md template
00189|     4. Potential: OpenCode plugin tracking Edit usage on code vs artifacts
00190|     
00191|     **Trigger pattern:** Failure-to-implementation. After agents fail, orchestrator tries to "just fix it" instead of trying different spawn strategy.
00192|     
00193|     ### 2. Self-Termination Attempts
00194|     
00195|     **What happens:** Spawned orchestrator tries to run `orch session end` or `/exit` instead of waiting for completion.
00196|     
00197|     **Root cause:** ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template contradicted the hierarchical completion model (told orchestrator to self-terminate).
00198|     
00199|     **Why it's wrong:** Breaks the "completed by level above" invariant. Orchestrator can't verify its own work from meta perspective.
00200|     
00201|     **Fix:** Template updated Jan 2026 to instruct "write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT".
00202|     
00203|     ### 3. Session Registry Drift
00204|     
00205|     **What happens:** `~/.orch/sessions.json` shows status "active" for completed sessions.
00206|     
00207|     **Root cause:** `orch complete` didn't update session registry status (only closed beads issues or removed registry entries).
00208|     
00209|     **Why it matters:** Stale active sessions accumulate, `orch status` shows ghost sessions, registry becomes unreliable.
00210|     
00211|     **Fix:** `orch complete` now updates status to "completed", `orch abandon` updates to "abandoned" - sessions preserved for history, not removed.
00212|

00217|   - Summary:
00218|     Trackpad clicks stop registering every ~15 minutes while cursor movement and keyboard continue working. `sudo killall -HUP WindowServer` fixes it every time (HUP = reconfigure, not restart). This points to WindowServer accumulating corrupted state in its click event pipeline. **Breakthrough in Session 15:** nuclear elimination of ~23 services stopped the freeze. Karabiner reinstalled (upgraded 14.13→15.9.0) — **no freeze with Karabiner running**, further confirming H2 elimination. NI HardwareAgent and Ollama fully uninstalled (not just disabled). System stable under full agent workload (3 concurrent spawns + Karabiner + OpenCode + orch). **Still narrowing:** ~18 user LaunchAgents + 3 system LaunchDaemons remain disabled. Next: re-enable in phases to isolate culprit.
00219|     
00220|     ---

00222|     ### Hypothesis 1: yabai event interception corrupts WindowServer state — ELIMINATED
00223|     
00224|     yabai uses the macOS Accessibility API to manage windows. Freeze recurred with yabai fully stopped (`yabai --stop-service`, confirmed no process via `pgrep`).
00225|

00229|     
00230|     Karabiner was fully uninstalled (app removed, DriverKit extension gone, no IOKit registry entries, no LaunchAgents). Freeze persisted immediately after fresh reboot with no Karabiner components present.
00231|     
00232|     **Evidence against:** Completely uninstalled — no process, no DriverKit extension (`systemextensionsctl list` clean), no IOKit entries (`ioreg` clean), no LaunchAgents. Freeze still occurred immediately post-reboot. Definitively eliminated.
00233|     
00234|     ### Hypothesis 3: WindowServer internal corruption (no external cause) — WEAKENED
00235|     
00236|     macOS 15.6.1 (Sequoia) may have a bug where WindowServer's click event routing table gets corrupted over time. This would be independent of any third-party software.
00237|     
00238|     **Evidence for:** Would explain why eliminating multiple apps in Session 11 didn't fix it.
00239|     
00240|     **Evidence against:** Three research probes (2026-02-11) searched GitHub, Reddit, Apple Discussions exhaustively — **zero matching reports** for this symptom pattern (clicks stop, cursor moves, HUP fixes). If this were a Sequoia bug, community reports would exist. This significantly weakens H3.
00241|     
00242|     ### Hypothesis 4: Memory pressure from OpenCode instance accumulation — WEAKENED
00243|     
00244|     OpenCode accumulates instances (with LSP/MCP/file watchers) per unique project directory. Each instance costs 300-500MB for LSP alone.

00248|     - This strongly suggests memory pressure is NOT the primary cause

00251|     **Evidence for (still relevant):**
00252|     - Memory pressure may be a contributing factor that lowers the threshold for the real culprit
00253|     - System was at 607MB free when freeze occurred in Session 14
00254|

00311| - Agent Lifecycle State Model
00312|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/agent-lifecycle-state-model.md
00313|   - Summary:
00314|     Agent state exists across **four independent layers** (tmux windows, OpenCode in-memory, OpenCode on-disk, beads comments). These layers fall into two distinct categories: **state layers** (beads, workspace files) that represent what work was done, and **infrastructure layers** (OpenCode sessions, tmux windows) that represent transient execution resources. The dashboard reconciles these via a **Priority Cascade**: check beads issue status first (highest authority), then Phase comments, then registry state, then session existence. Status can appear "wrong" at the dashboard level while being "correct" at each individual layer - this is a measurement artifact from combining multiple sources of truth.
00315|     
00316|     ---
00317|   - Critical Invariants:
00318|     1. **Phase: Complete is agent's declaration** - Only agent can reach this, not orchestrator
00319|     2. **Beads issue closed = canonical completion** - All status queries defer to beads
00320|     3. **Session existence ≠ agent still working** - Sessions persist indefinitely
00321|     4. **Status checks don't mutate state** - Calculation is read-only, no side effects
00322|     5. **Multiple sources must be reconciled** - No single source has complete truth
00323|     6. **Tmux windows are UI layer only** - Not authoritative for state
00324|     
00325|     ---
00326|   - Why This Fails:
00327|     ### Failure Mode 1: Dashboard Shows "Active" When Agent is Done
00328|     
00329|     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows agent as active, but `bd show <id>` says status=closed
00330|     
00331|     **Root cause:** Dashboard caching or SSE lag - hasn't received beads update yet
00332|     
00333|     **Why it happens:**
00334|     - Agent reaches Phase: Complete
00335|     - `orch complete` closes beads issue
00336|     - Beads issue status = closed
00337|     - Dashboard hasn't refreshed or polled beads yet
00338|     - Dashboard still shows cached "active" state
00339|     
00340|     **Fix:** Refresh dashboard browser tab (forces beads query)
00341|     
00342|     **NOT the fix:** Deleting OpenCode session (treats symptom, not cause)
00343|     
00344|     ### Failure Mode 2: "Dead" Agents That Actually Completed
00345|     
00346|     **Symptom:** Dashboard shows "dead", but work is done and beads issue closed
00347|     
00348|     **Root cause:** Session cleanup happened before dashboard queried, cascade reached session check
00349|     
00350|     **Why it happens:**
00351|     - Agent completed, beads issue closed
00352|     - Session cleanup ran (manual or automatic)
00353|     - Dashboard cascade: beads check → no issue (closed) → session check → no session → "dead"
00354|     
00355|     **Fix (Jan 8):** Priority Cascade puts beads/Phase check before session existence check
00356|     
00357|     ### Failure Mode 3: Agent Went Idle But Not Complete
00358|     
00359|     **Symptom:** Session status is "idle" but no `Phase: Complete` comment
00360|     
00361|     **Root cause:** Agent ran out of context, crashed, or didn't follow completion protocol
00362|     
00363|     **Why it happens:**
00364|     - Session exhausts context (150k tokens)
00365|     - Agent stops responding
00366|     - SSE event: `session.status = idle`
00367|     - No `Phase: Complete` was ever written

00391| - Beads SQLite Database Corruption
00392|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/beads-database-corruption.md
00393|   - Summary:
00394|     Beads SQLite corruption occurs when the daemon enters a **rapid restart loop** (any failure → retry → fail → retry). Each cycle opens/closes the database performing WAL checkpoint. High-frequency checkpoints across unstable conditions (sandbox filesystem, legacy validation, any daemon failure) create opportunities for incomplete WAL operations, manifesting as **0-byte WAL files** that corrupt the database. The fix is **preventing rapid restarts**, not fixing individual failure causes.
00395|     
00396|     ---
00397|   - Why This Fails:
00398|     ### 1. No Backoff on Daemon Failure
00399|     
00400|     **What happens:** Daemon fails → restarts immediately → fails → restarts → cycles indefinitely.
00401|     
00402|     **Root cause:** No exponential backoff between restart attempts. launchd `KeepAlive` causes immediate restart.
00403|     
00404|     **Why detection is hard:** Each individual failure looks like "bad luck" - only aggregate pattern reveals problem.
00405|     
00406|     **Fix:** Implement minimum interval between daemon starts (e.g., 30 seconds).
00407|     
00408|     ### 2. Sandbox Environment Not Detected Early
00409|     
00410|     **What happens:** Daemon starts inside Claude Code sandbox, tries to chmod socket, fails, but has already opened database.
00411|     
00412|     **Root cause:** Sandbox detection happens AFTER database open, not before daemon start.
00413|     
00414|     **Fix:** Detect sandbox at CLI entry point, skip daemon auto-start entirely.
00415|     
00416|     ### 3. Legacy Database Validation Fails Late
00417|     
00418|     **What happens:** Database opens successfully, WAL enabled, THEN fingerprint validation fails.
00419|     
00420|     **Root cause:** Validation is post-open check, not pre-open gate.
00421|     
00422|     **Fix:** Check fingerprint before enabling WAL mode.
00423|     
00424|     ### 4. No Health Gate Before Operations
00425|     
00426|     **What happens:** Daemon starts despite known-bad state (missing fingerprint, sandbox environment).
00427|     
00428|     **Root cause:** No pre-flight checks before daemon entry point.
00429|     
00430|     **Fix:** `bd daemon start` should validate prerequisites before proceeding.

00069| ```
00070| **Use for:** Normal workflow, high concurrency, batch processing
00071| 
00072| **Characteristics:**
00073| - Headless (no tmux window)
00074| - High concurrency (5+ agents)
00075| - Depends on OpenCode server (localhost:4096)
00076| - Daemon-managed lifecycle
00077| 
00078| ### Escape Hatch (Manual + Claude CLI)
00079| ```bash
00080| orch spawn --bypass-triage --mode claude --model opus --tmux feature-impl "task" --issue ID
00081| ```
00082| **Use for:**
00083| - 🔥 Building infrastructure the primary path depends on
00084| - 🔧 Debugging when OpenCode server is unstable
00085| - 🎯 Critical work that can't afford to lose progress to crashes
00086| - 👁️ Work requiring visual monitoring
00087| 
00088| **Characteristics:**
00089| - Tmux window (visible progress)
00090| - Independent of OpenCode server
00091| - Crash-resistant (agents survive service restarts)
00092| - Manual lifecycle management
00093| 
00094| ### Architectural Principle: Critical Paths Need Escape Hatches
00095| 
00096| **Pattern discovered Jan 10, 2026:** When building observability infrastructure, OpenCode server crashed repeatedly (3 times in 1 hour), killing all agents working on the fixes. Switched to `--mode claude --tmux` for critical agents (orch doctor, overmind supervision, dashboard integration), which survived crashes and completed the work.
00097| 
00098| **General rule:** When infrastructure can fail, critical paths need independent secondary paths that:
00099| 1. **Don't depend on what failed** (claude CLI ≠ opencode server)
00100| 2. **Provide visibility** (tmux vs headless)
00101| 3. **Can complete the work** (opus for quality)
00102| 
00103| ### Architectural Principle: Pain as Signal
00104| 
00105| **Pattern discovered Jan 17, 2026:** Autonomous error correction requires agents to "feel" the friction of their own failure in real-time. Passive logs/metrics are insufficient for agent self-healing.
00106| 1. **Infrastructure Injection:** System-level sensors (coaching plugins) inject detections (loops, thrashing) directly into the agent's sensory stream.
00107| 2. **Pressure over Compensation:** Friction is injected as tool-layer messages, forcing the agent to confront its own degradation rather than relying on human babysitting.
00108| 
00109| **See:** `.kb/guides/resilient-infrastructure-patterns.md` for implementation patterns
00110| 
00111| ## Key References
00112| 
00113| **Before debugging, check the relevant guide in `.kb/guides/`:**
00114| 
00115| | Topic | Guide | When to Read |
00116| |-------|-------|--------------|
00117| | Agent lifecycle | `agent-lifecycle.md` | Agents not completing, dashboard wrong |
00118| | Spawn | `spawn.md` | Spawn failures, wrong context, flags |
00119| | Status/Dashboard | `status-dashboard.md` | Wrong status, dashboard issues |
00120| | Beads integration | `beads-integration.md` | bd commands failing, issue tracking |
00121| | Skill system | `skill-system.md` | Skill not loading, wrong behavior |
00122| | Daemon | `daemon.md` | Auto-spawn issues, triage workflow |
00123| | Resilient infrastructure | `resilient-infrastructure-patterns.md` | Building/fixing critical infrastructure, escape hatches |
00124| 
00125| These guides synthesize 280+ investigations into authoritative references. Created Jan 4, 2026 after repeatedly re-investigating documented problems.
00126| 
00127| ## Dashboard Server Management
00128| 
00129| **Always use `orch-dashboard` script** - handles orphan cleanup, stale sockets, and proper startup:
00130| 
00131| ```bash
00132| orch-dashboard start    # Start all services (kills orphans first)
00133| orch-dashboard stop     # Stop all services
00134| orch-dashboard restart  # Full restart with cleanup
00135| orch-dashboard status   # Check service status
00136| orch-dashboard logs     # View service logs (overmind echo)
00137| ```
00138| 
00139| **Service ports:** OpenCode (4096), orch serve (3348), Web UI (5188)
00140| 
00141| **Dashboard URL:** http://localhost:5188
00142| 
00143| **Why not raw overmind?** Direct `overmind start` can fail silently when orphan processes hold ports or stale sockets exist. The `orch-dashboard` script handles these edge cases.
00144| 
00145| **Production:** Future VPS deployment will use systemd. See `.kb/decisions/2026-01-10-dev-vs-prod-architecture.md`.
00146| 
00147| ## Key Packages
00148| 
00149| ### cmd/orch/main.go (Entry Point)
00150| - Uses Cobra framework for CLI structure
00151| - All commands defined inline (spawn, status, complete, send, etc.)
00152| - Global `--server` flag for OpenCode URL
00153| - Subcommand groups: `account`, `daemon`
00154| 
00155| ### pkg/opencode/ (OpenCode Client)
00156| - `Client` struct with HTTP methods for OpenCode REST API
00157| - `ListSessions()`, `GetSession()`, `CreateSession()`, `GetMessages()`
00158| - `SSEClient` for real-time event streaming
00159| - Session status polling for completion detection
00160| - `ExtractRecentText()` for extracting text from message history
00161| 
00162| ### pkg/model/ (Model Resolution)
00163| - `Resolve(spec)` maps aliases to full provider/model format
00164| - Aliases: `opus`, `sonnet`, `haiku` (Anthropic), `flash`, `pro` (Gemini)
00165| - Default: `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` (Opus restricted to Claude Code as of Jan 2026)
00166| - Aliases: `opus`, `sonnet`, `haiku` (Anthropic), `flash`, `pro` (Gemini)
00167| - Default: `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` (Opus restricted to Claude Code as of Jan 2026)
00168| - Aliases: `opus`, `sonnet`, `haiku` (Anthropic), `flash`, `pro` (Gemini)
00169| - Default: `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` (Opus restricted to Claude Code as of Jan 2026)
00170| 
00171| ### pkg/account/ (Account Management)
00172| - `LoadConfig()` reads `~/.orch/accounts.yaml`
00173| - `Switch(name)` refreshes OAuth tokens and updates OpenCode auth
00174| - Token sources: OpenCode auth file, macOS Keychain
00175| - Same config format as Python orch-cli for interop
00176| 
00177| ### pkg/spawn/ (Spawn Context)
00178| - `SpawnConfig` struct with all spawn parameters
00179| - `GenerateContext()` creates SPAWN_CONTEXT.md content
00180| - Embeds skill content, task description, beads issue context
00181| - Sets deliverables paths for verification
00182| - Conditionally includes server context for UI-focused skills (feature-impl, systematic-debugging, reliability-testing)
00183| 
00184| ### pkg/config/ (Project Config)
00185| - `Load()` reads `.orch/config.yaml` from project directory
00186| - `Config.Servers` maps service names to ports (e.g., `web: 5173`)
00187| - Used by `orch servers` commands and spawn context generation
00188| 
00189| ### pkg/verify/ (Completion Verification)
00190| - `Check()` validates agent work before closing
00191| - Verifies: Phase Complete, deliverables exist, commits present
00192| - `Update()` closes beads issue with completion reason
00193| 
00194| ## Spawn Flow
00195| 
00196| 1. `orch spawn SKILL "task"` invokes spawn command in main.go
00197| 2. Resolves model alias via `pkg/model.Resolve()`
00198| 3. Creates workspace: `.orch/workspace/{name}/`
00199| 4. Generates `SPAWN_CONTEXT.md` via `pkg/spawn`
00200| 5. **Default (headless):** Creates session via HTTP API, sends prompt
00201| 6. **With --tmux:** Creates session + tmux window for monitoring (opt-in)
00202| 7. **With --inline:** Runs OpenCode TUI in current terminal (blocking)
00203| 8. Returns immediately (unless --inline)
00204| 
00205| ## Commands
00206| 
00207| ### Agent Lifecycle
00208| - `spawn <skill> "task"` - Create agent with skill context
00209| - `status` - List active agents
00210| - `send <session-id> "message"` - Q&A on existing session
00211| - `complete <agent-id>` - Verify and close agent work
00212| - `abandon <agent-id>` - Mark stuck agent as abandoned
00213| - `clean` - Remove completed agents from registry
00214| 
00215| ### Monitoring
00216| - `monitor` - Real-time SSE event watching
00217| - `wait <agent-id>` - Block until phase reached
00218| - `tail <agent-id>` - Capture recent tmux output (requires `--tmux` spawn)
00219| - `question <agent-id>` - Extract pending question
00220| - `serve` - HTTP API server for web UI (port 3348)
00221| 
00222| ### Account & Model
00223| - `account list` - Show saved Claude Max accounts
00224| - `account switch <name>` - Switch to different account
00225| - `account remove <name>` - Remove saved account
00226| - `usage` - Show Claude Max usage (delegates to Python)
00227| 
00228| ### Automation
00229| - `work <issue-id>` - Spawn from beads issue with skill inference
00230| - `daemon run` - Run autonomous processing in foreground
00231| - `daemon preview` - Show what would be spawned
00232| 
00233| ### Server Management
00234| - `servers list` - Show all projects with port allocations and running status
00235| - `servers start <project>` - Start servers via tmuxinator
00236| - `servers stop <project>` - Stop servers for a project
00237| - `servers attach <project>` - Attach to servers window
00238| - `servers open <project>` - Open web port in browser
00239| - `servers status` - Show summary view (running/stopped counts)
00240| 
00241| ## Development
00242| 
00243| ```bash
00244| # Build
00245| make build
00246| 
00247| # Test
00248| make test
00249| 
00250| # Install to ~/bin/orch
00251| make install
00252| 
00253| # Verify version
00254| orch version
00255| ```
00256| 
00257| ### Adding New Commands
00258| 
00259| 1. Add command to `cmd/orch/main.go` (inline with Cobra)
00260| 2. Or create `cmd/orch/{name}.go` for complex commands
00261| 3. Add to `rootCmd.AddCommand()` in init()
00262| 
00263| ### Adding New Packages
00264| 
00265| 1. Create `pkg/{name}/{name}.go`
00266| 2. Create `pkg/{name}/{name}_test.go`
00267| 3. Import in cmd/orch as needed
00268| 
00269| ## Gotchas
00270| 
00271| - **Window targeting**: Use workspace name, not window index
00272| - **Model default**: Opus (Max subscription), not Gemini (pay-per-token)
00273| - **SSE parsing**: Event type is inside JSON data, not `event:` prefix
00274| - **Beads integration**: Shells out to `bd` CLI, doesn't use API directly
00275| - **OpenCode auth**: Reads from `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json`
00276| 
00277| ## Common Commands
00278| 
00279| ```bash
00280| # Spawn with specific model (headless by default)
00281| orch spawn --model flash investigation "explore X"
00282| 
00283| # Spawn in tmux window (opt-in for visual monitoring)
00284| orch spawn --tmux investigation "explore X"
00285| 
00286| # Run inline with TUI (blocking)
00287| orch spawn --inline investigation "explore X"
00288| 
00289| # Switch accounts when rate-limited
00290| orch account switch work
00291| 
00292| # Spawn from beads issue
00293| orch spawn feature-impl "implement X" --issue proj-123
00294| 
00295| # Wait for agent to complete
00296| orch wait proj-123 --timeout 30m
00297| 
00298| # Complete and verify agent work
00299| orch complete proj-123
00300| 
00301| # Clean up finished agents
00302| orch clean
00303| ```
00304| 
00305| ## Event Tracking
00306| 
00307| Agent lifecycle events are logged to `~/.orch/events.jsonl` for stats aggregation.
00308| 
00309| ### Event Types
00310| 
00311| | Event | Source | Purpose |
00312| |-------|--------|---------|
00313| | `session.spawned` | `orch spawn` | Agent created |
00314| | `agent.completed` | `orch complete` or `bd close` hook | Agent finished work |
00315| | `agent.abandoned` | `orch abandon` | Agent abandoned |
00316| 
00317| ### Beads Close Hook
00318| 
00319| When issues are closed directly via `bd close` (bypassing `orch complete`), the beads hook at `.beads/hooks/on_close` emits an `agent.completed` event. This closes the tracking gap.
00320| 
00321| **Hook location:** `.beads/hooks/on_close` (project-specific)
00322| 
00323| **Manual event emission:**
00324| ```bash
00325| # Emit completion event directly (used by hooks)
00326| orch emit agent.completed --beads-id proj-123 --reason "Closed via bd close"
00327| ```
00328| 
00329| **To enable in a project:**
00330| 1. Create `.beads/hooks/on_close` (executable)
00331| 2. Copy content from orch-go's hook as a template
00332|
